rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ROBERT J. SIGMUND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No personal representative has been appointed yet for the deceased conservatee's estate. (2) Another fee petition has been filed (by the court-appointed attorney), so it is unclear if this estate should be terminated at this time. (3) The fee declaration filed by the attorney for the conservator of the person appears to have just been filed on 5/25/23; no courtesy copy has been received yet by the assigned examiner as of 6/9/23. (4) Is there an older accounting from the trustee of the Jane Sigmund trust, Faith Cleary, that shows the approximate value of that testamentary trust? (5) The disbursements reflect tow reimbursements of $94.11 to the conservator of the person for Amazon purchases made on 8/15/22. Was one a duplicate payment? (6) Why was the conservator of the person reimbursed for Uber rides on 8/11/22? (7) The Comcast/Xfinity monthly bills appear to be quite high. What services were included? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 8/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There is no need to narrate or list in a petition all of the services provided by the attorney for the fiduciary, especially for routine estate management activities such as preparing accounting petitions, reviewing and filing bank statements, reviewing the court investigator's report, and paying the assessment fee or communicating with other attorneys and parties; only exceptional services should be highlighted so they stand out.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM MICHAEL HENRY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Court Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Retention Of Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/25/2023. 1) No proposed order has been received. A copy of the settlement agreement must be attached to the proposed order, with the Court's signature line provided on the last attachment page. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Donna Echiverri-Beckham's address listed on the proof of service is different from her address previously reported to the Court. 4) The information required by LRSF 14.43(D)(1)-(3) must be provided. 5) CCP section 664.6(a) requires parties to "pending litigation" to stipulate, but the underlying petitions were both taken off calendar at the 02/28/2023 hearing. Do the parties to the settlement agreement qualify as parties to pending litigation entitled to request the Court's retention of jurisdiction to enforce the settlement? 6) Was the petitioner serving as trustee when this petition was filed? If not, please provide the date(s) of the petitioner's resignation as trustee and appointment of Carole Sullivan as successor trustee. 7) The only heir who signed the settlement agreement is Robert Bruce Smith. Did he have authority to bind the other heirs to the terms of the settlement agreement as attorney-in-fact under a power of attorney or otherwise? 8) Should the heirs who are not beneficiaries of the trust and did not sign the settlement agreement be required to be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq.?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LILLIAN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although the amended petition filed by Ray Torres is not procedurally ready to be heard. 1. No proof of service of the Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. 2. No proof of publication of the Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. 3. The caption of the amended petition is incomplete as is item 2c; however, if he intended to seek no IAEA authority whatsoever, this is not an issue. 4. Item 3g(2) is not correct, as a "life partner" does not have standing and a creditor does not have priority before a decedent's sibling or even the public administrator. [Probate Code Section 8561(q).] 5. Item 8 of the petition is incomplete: the names and dates of death of the predeceased parents and the petitioner himself, who is the person named in item 2 of the amended petition, are missing. [LRSF 14.18 D.] 6. Still no proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PROCEDURAL ISSUES on Trust Petition: 1. Clarification of the relief. Prayer appears broad and ambiguous as it does not set forth which provision needs to be reformed and how. 2. Notice of Hearing and a copy of petition to be directly served on minors pursuant to Prob. Code 17203 and CRC 7.51. Petitioners sent the notice of hearing to them in c/o. 3. Response to Tentative Ruling filed by Dowke, Cheong and Sekijiman on 4/10/23; suggesting a potential amended petition. Said document is not reviewed as it is not. before the court. On Objection: 1. Proof of service of opposition to meet CRC 7.51. Minor beneficiaries need to be directly noticed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ELSBET GRUEN FAMILY TRUST, DATED DECEMBER 14, TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Breach Of Trust; Immediate Suspension Of Trustee; Removal Of Trustee; Appointment Of Successor; An Account; Surcharge Of Trustee; Breach
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/1/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) No proof of service of notice to all trust beneficiaries has been filed. (4) The copy of the trust attached to the petition has pages out of order. (5) Furthermore, the trust attached to the petition does not show the petitioner as a current beneficiary. Upon the death of the grantor, the trust was to be divided int two parts: Claude Gruen is to receive all net income and discretionary principal of the GST Exemption amount during his life and the remainder (second part) outright. The grandchildren only receive any remaining trust estate after the death of Claude Gruen and Nina Gruen. (6) It is unclear who the persons listed in paragraph 78 are, but if they are entitled to notice, they must be given direct notice. [CRC 7.51(a).] Notice may not be mailed in care of another unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. Except as permitted in Probate Code section 1460.1 for guardianships, conservatorships, and certain protective proceedings under division 4 of the Probate Code, notice to a minor must be sent directly to the minor. A separate copy of the notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] The proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LATONYA GRACE ROGERS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Existence And Internal Affairs Of A Trust (Prob.C.§17200) And To Determine Whether Successor Trustee John David Barry Shall Be
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2023 (no earlier date available). The following issues were e-mailed to counsel on 06/06/2023: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No proof of service is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(1) must also be attached. 4) The petition must state the name and address of each person entitled to notice. Probate Code section 17201. 5) Does the petitioner have a copy of the case name trust? If so, please provide a copy. 6) Did the settlor of the case name trust (Latonya Rogers) leave a will? If so, please provide a copy. 7) If the petitioner is not a current or former beneficiary or trustee of the case name trust, does the petitioner have standing to seek relief under Probate Code section 17200(a) concerning the existence or internal affairs of that trust? 8) If no conservator has been appointed for the settlor of the case name trust, does the probate department have concurrent jurisdiction over the financial abuse claim under section 15657.3 of the Welfare & Institutions Code? Even if the probate department is to accept the jurisdiction, no summons has been issued and served on John Barry. 9) If the petitioner is requesting an order directing John Barry to transfer the subject real property (or proceeds if sold) to the trustee of Eddie Rogers' trust (or to the personal representative of the estate of Latonya Rogers), should the petitioner be required to amend the petition to seek the relief under Probate Code sections 850 & 856?

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] When did the decedentâs parents die? When did the other brother die? A supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to address these issues. A courtesy copy of the supplement must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRED LAZARUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2023 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 06/06/2023). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 06/05/2023.
